text.title: user-research-at-scale Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for user-research-at-scale as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Running sentiment analysis on non-English transcripts without routing by language.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Sentiment models miss sarcasm, code-switching, and culture-specific affect in non-English text. Polarity flips on negation in low-resource languages without language-aware routing.

failure-mode.description: Letting the theme-coder invent codes when the codebook is unclear rather than using the proposed_codes overflow channel.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Invented codes contaminate the taxonomy and produce theme drift across batches. Strict allow-list enforcement with a separate proposed_codes channel keeps the codebook clean.

failure-mode.description: Hallucinating participant IDs in the report when citing quotes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents invent participant IDs that do not exist in the corpus. Require verbatim span offsets (start_char, end_char) tied to a specific session file, not paraphrases.

failure-mode.description: Re-running coding on the same transcript without caching, burning 10x tokens.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Hash the transcript content and cache the coded output. Skip transcripts that already have a cached result. Uncached re-runs are pure waste.

failure-mode.description: Feeding full transcript bundles > 200k tokens into a long-context model without chunking.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Long-context models silently truncate at token boundaries. Chunk at session boundaries with overlap and verify completeness before synthesis.

failure-mode.description: Pattern-analyst over-indexing on the first segment in the prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs weight earlier context more heavily. Randomize segment order across runs and compare findings to detect position bias.

failure-mode.description: Mixing timestamp formats from different platforms (Maze milliseconds, UserTesting ISO, Otter seconds) without normalization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Mixed timestamps break quote offset matching and prevent cross-platform de-duplication. Normalize in the transcript-cleaner stage before any downstream use.

failure-mode.description: Agents quietly dropping non-English sessions when the prompt does not explicitly handle them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Silently dropping sessions produces a biased corpus. Assert language coverage in tests and route non-English sessions to a translation step before coding.
